author: @selfcorrected-Bobby
text: Have you thought of making a period film acting as Faraday and sir Anthon Hopkins as your father? I would gladly write a script for the screenplay if it is a project tha sir Hopkins and BBC or a film studio might take to heart. furthermore  / additionally, an LLM avatar can be made of all your books, videos, movements, etc. so that all your video and audio creates a virtual simulacrum of you. To it added could be some school textbooks so that students learn from you, you do experiments, though in your series. This can be another product for you, an educational Tool. It is reaching the stage where such an interactive learning environment can be made where children go to virtual school and communicate with a virtual you, only it is a simulacrum,   but if kids have real problems then they are listed to connect with you personally if the LLM avatar doesn't cut it.... virtual learning. will you have a think about my idea as a possibility, or the movie? The future is upon us! it could be done with .gov or some business company edutech company I know ed Tech companies (I know some of them, i.e. TAL, is listed on the NYSE), and could be worth approaching.

author: @TsetTsyung
text:
I've been using Freecad on n off for a couple of years and love it. As I'm hoping to do my own 3D printed and laser engraving/cut designs it's so good due to its free price tag.

BRW, check out the Laser Cut Interlocking workbench - it's so cool!

replies.author: @dalewheat
replies.text: Little update:  I tried to install the gears workbench addon in FreeCAD 1.0.0 and it installed but threw an error when trying to switch to the workbench: "Error importing numpy: …”.  I upgraded to FreeCAD 1.0.1 and it works as advertised.  I’m really looking forward to playing with this tool and thank you for bringing it to my attention!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering

author: @chrisbingham3289
text: 🇬🇧Brit here 🇬🇧. Hi Robert love the fact you finely got round to FreeCad and I am pleased you have found it useful. Been using for about 10 years  now wouldn't use anything else. Keep up your brilliant ideas. 👌

replies.author: @yasirrakhurrafat1142
replies.text:
I've heard Autocad/Fusion is great for wiring and junction modeling. And solidworks great for lots of fluid and otherwise simulations.
I think freecad is awesome for sure though.
Those other things don't have open source equivalents afaik.

replies.author: @chrisbingham3289
replies.text: @yasirrakhurrafat1142 That's one of the reasons I use it, the others are you get to keep the soft wear and your files are stored on your own computer so always available .

replies.author: @yasirrakhurrafat1142
replies.text:
​@chrisbingham3289 oh yeah.
Others could very well be snooping and prying into your projects, possibly more.
But the functionalities are too useful in some fields, for me to ignore.
Although I'm just starting out, I have big ambitions that may someday require use of the simulation and other functionalities.
